_description: Ecosystem service values — per canopy-acre per year
_sources: Carbon: EPA SC-GHG 2023 ($190/tonne CO2, 2% near-term) × 2.6 t/acre/yr (Atlanta iTree Eco 2014)
Stormwater: Benefit transfer from USDA CUFR / iTree Eco literature
Air quality: Nowak et al. 2014, total pollutant removal value (PM2.5/O3/NO2/SO2)
Energy: McPherson 2003, Atlanta iTree Eco — 1,800 kWh/acre/yr × $0.14/kWh
Habitat: Benefit transfer from ecosystem services literature — $320/acre canopy
Property: Kovacs et al. 2022 — ~7% premium (applied to market value, capped at 12%)

carbon.label: Carbon Sequestration
carbon.methodology: 2.6 t CO2/acre/yr × $190/tonne (EPA SC-GHG 2023, 2% near-term discount rate)
carbon.source: Atlanta iTree Eco 2014; EPA Social Cost of Greenhouse Gases 2023 (Table ES-1)

stormwater.label: Stormwater Management
stormwater.methodology: Benefit transfer from USDA CUFR / iTree Eco urban canopy valuation literature
stormwater.source: USDA Center for Urban Forestry Research; iTree Eco model outputs

airQuality.label: Air Quality Improvement
airQuality.methodology: Total pollutant removal value (PM2.5, O3, NO2, SO2) × BenMAP health valuation

energy.label: Energy Savings
energy.source: McPherson 2003; Atlanta iTree Eco; GA Power avg residential rate

habitat.label: Habitat Value
habitat.methodology: Benefit transfer from ecosystem services valuation literature
habitat.source: Troy & Wilson 2006; Brander & Koetse 2011 (approximate, benefit transfer)

propertyPremium.label: Property Value Premium
propertyPremium.methodology: ~7% premium for mature canopy coverage (meta-analysis, approximate)
propertyPremium.source: Kovacs et al. 2022; Netusil et al. 2014
